meta.reportLabel: Fri PM update — REVERSAL: Canada jobs BEAT (+18.2k vs ~+10k, unemp 6.5% from 6.6%) fired CAD and broke the USD/CAD uptrend to a 2-wk low ~1.4136; a softer USD (DXY 101->100.9, fading Fed-hike bets) lifted EUR/USD to ~1.1420. Trump says Iran 'reached out to make a deal' - de-escalation tilt; WTI soft <$72, gold firms back >$4,100 (~$4,115). Both morning USD-leg trades wrong-footed; board now all-RANGE. Jul 14 CPI the single arbiter.
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40 + XAU + XAG + USOIL
meta.regime: The de-escalation tilt + a softer USD wrong-footed the morning's two USD-leg trades. Canada's June jobs beat (+18.2k vs ~+10k, unemployment down to 6.5%) fired CAD and broke the USD/CAD uptrend to ~1.4136 despite hollow internals (all part-time, goods -43.7k); fading Fed-hike bets dropped DXY to ~100.9 and lifted EUR/USD to ~1.1420. Trump said Iran 'reached out to make a deal' as regional mediators work to reconvene Doha talks, keeping WTI soft under $72 and letting gold firm back above $4,100. The board is now almost entirely RANGE - the USD bid is leaking, not broken, and Jul 14 US CPI is the single arbiter.
meta.nextBigEvent: Tue Jul 14, 12:30 UTC: US June CPI - the 4.2%-regime check that decides the Sept hike (~45-50%) and whether the leaking USD bid snaps back or breaks.

dailyRead: The morning's two USD-leg trades got wrong-footed by lunchtime. Canada's June jobs BEAT (+18.2k vs ~+10k consensus, unemployment DOWN to 6.5% from 6.6%) firmed CAD and knocked USD/CAD to a two-week low ~1.4136 - its first weekly loss in six - even though the internals were soft (all part-time, goods-producing shed 43.7k, manufacturing -17k). At the same time a softer USD (DXY back to ~100.9, fading Fed-hike bets) lifted EUR/USD to ~1.1420, so the morning's short-EUR/USD + long-USD/CAD pair both worked against the tape. The driver is de-escalation hope: Trump said Iran 'reached out to make a deal,' and Qatar/Pakistan/Oman/Turkey/Egypt/Saudi mediators are working to reconvene talks even as CENTCOM's Jul 8-9 strikes (80+ targets, 60+ IRGC boats) leave the MoU 'over.' That let WTI stay soft under $72 (Brent ~$76) and gold firm back above $4,100 (~$4,115). Net: the board is now almost entirely RANGE, the USD bid is leaking, and Jul 14 US CPI is the single arbiter - stand down size into it.

today.headline: Jobs BEAT (+18.2k vs +10k, unemp 6.5%) fires CAD; USD/CAD to ~1.4136
today.read: The beat + falling unemployment broke the July USD/CAD uptrend (first weekly loss in six), but the internals are hollow - all part-time, goods-producing shed 43.7k, manufacturing -17k. Fade extreme CAD strength: the BoC stays sidelined Jul 15 and the oil floor is still eroding.

today.headline: Bid LEAKING - DXY 101->100.9 as Fed-hike bets fade
today.read: De-escalation hope + the soft NFP crack outweighed the hawkish SEP dots today, so the higher-for-longer premium leaked. Still 1-2 hikes penciled and zero cuts - a hot Jul 14 CPI snaps the dollar straight back; a second soft print breaks it.

today.headline: EUR/USD up to ~1.1420 - on the soft USD, not EUR strength
today.read: Cooling flash 2.8% still guts Jul 23 urgency while core 2.5% keeps a Sept hike alive; today's move was entirely the leaking USD bid. A hot US CPI reverses it; a hawkish ECB core tone would give EUR its own leg.

today.headline: Gold firms back above $4,100 (~$4,115) as yields ease
today.read: Off Wednesday's ~$4,030 low: the softer USD and easing oil-yield pressure let gold steady into a little-changed week. Still RANGE - a hot CPI re-arms the real-rate selloff; a ceasefire that drops yields while keeping a haven bid revives the long.

today.headline: Softer US yields let spot ease toward ~161.5
today.read: The yield prop relaxed with the leaking USD bid, giving a mild short-USD/JPY tilt on top of the MoF-backstop asymmetry (Y11.73tn precedent). A yields re-spike on a hot CPI drags spot to 163+ and forces the MoF's hand.

today.headline: De-escalation risk bid + soft USD lift the beta
today.read: AUD caught a de-escalation bid, but the Aug 11 hike tail is thin (~36%) and soft China CPI (+1%) caps the demand pulse - the soft USD is doing most of the AUD/USD work. A Hormuz re-escalation flips it risk-off; Q2 CPI late Jul is the re-arm.

today.headline: No change - dovish-hike NZD, higher hurdle for the next move
today.read: The 6-0 hike to 2.50% was scripted dovish (inflation seen peaked 3.9% Q2); NZ Q2 CPI ~Jul 16 is the swing factor. Soft USD offsets the dovish leg, keeping NZD/USD RANGE. A 4%+ CPI re-arms the hawks.

today.headline: No change - firm and quiet; soft USD adds a mild long tilt
today.read: Services CPI 3.7% keeps both hike dissents alive into Jul 30, and the leaking USD bid gives GBP/USD a mild long tilt >1.34. Firm but rangebound; a hot US CPI re-firms the dollar against it.

strength.tag: DXY 101->100.9; Fed-hike bets fade, SEP still 1-2 hikes
strength.drivers: CPI 4.2% headline / core 2.9% (May); June NFP +57k big miss (unemp 4.2%); Fed 3.50-3.75% hawkish hold, SEP pencils 1-2 hikes and zero 2026 cuts, Sept ~45-50%. Today the market faded the hike odds (DXY to ~100.9) as de-escalation hope + the NFP crack outweighed the hawkish dots.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Hawkish hold into the Jul 28-29 FOMC, but the bid is leaking - the SEP caps the downside while Jul 14 CPI is the only trigger that resets direction. Risk: a hot CPI snaps the dollar back; a second soft print turns the NFP crack into a trend. Next FOMC Jul 28-29.

strength.tag: services CPI 3.7%; soft USD adds a mild long tilt
strength.drivers: Core 2.6% (May); services CPI 3.7% (up from 3.2%); headline 2.8% held; DMP wages 4.2%. BoE 3.75%, hawkish 7-2 hold (2 hike votes); post-Starmer political calm + the leaking USD bid support GBP/USD >1.34.
strength.forward: 🦅 Hawkish - the services re-acceleration keeps the hike tail and both dissents alive into the Jul 30 MPR round. Risk: a soft activity/labor wave re-splits the MPC; a hot US CPI re-firms the dollar against it. Next BoE Jul 30.

strength.tag: 2.50%, first hike since 2023; higher hurdle next
strength.drivers: RBNZ hiked 25bp to 2.50% Jul 8, 6-0 - but the statement was the dovish version: headline inflation now seen PEAKED at 3.9% in Q2 (easing to 3.3% Q3), a 'considerably higher hurdle' for the next move. Soft China CPI (+1%) trims the growth story; the soft USD offset the dovish leg today.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Hike delivered but guidance capped it - Sept still likely, not aggressively priced; Aug 19 should be a hold. NZ Q2 CPI (~Jul 16) validates or breaks the 'peaked at 3.9%' framing. Risk: a 4%+ CPI print re-arms the hawks; risk-off beta cuts the other way. Next RBNZ Aug 19.

strength.tag: spot ~161.5; softer US yields ease the pin, MoF still the story
strength.drivers: Core CPI 1.4% (May, subsidy-suppressed); unemp 2.5%, jobs/applicants 1.26 tight. BoJ 1.00% (hiked Jun 16, 7-1), guides more hikes. Softer US yields today let spot slip toward ~161.5 - still deep in the proven MoF zone (Y11.73tn/$74bn precedent) with no intervention yet.
strength.forward: 🦅 The haven bid is real and the yield prop eased today, giving a mild short-USD/JPY tilt on top of the backstop asymmetry; BoJ Jul 31 stays hawkish. Risk: a yields re-spike to 163+ forces the MoF's hand; fast de-escalation restores the carry grind. Next BoJ Jul 31.

strength.tag: thin Aug tail (~36%), China CPI +1%, soft USD lift
strength.drivers: May CPI: trimmed mean 3.6%; headline 4.0%; unemp 4.5% loosening (June jobs Jul 16). RBA 4.35% (held Jun after 75bp of 2026 hikes); the Aug 11 hike was priced only ~36%. China CPI missed at +1%, trimming the demand pulse; de-escalation hope + the soft USD lifted the beta today.
strength.forward: ⚖️🔻 The sticky-core floor stands but the thin hike tail + soft China cap it - the soft USD is doing most of the AUD/USD work. Risk: a Hormuz re-escalation flips risk-off; a durable de-escalation plus a hot Q2 CPI (late Jul) flips it hawkish. Next RBA Aug 11.

strength.tag: flash 2.8% cooling BUT core 2.5%; EUR/USD up on USD
strength.drivers: EZ June flash HICP 2.8% (down from 3.2%) BUT core ROSE to 2.5% from 2.2%. Unemp 6.3%. ECB 2.25% depo (hiked Jun 11), meeting-by-meeting; EUR/USD rose to ~1.1420 today purely on the softer USD, not EUR strength.
strength.forward: ⚖️ Cooling headline guts Jul 23 urgency (hold expected) but rising core keeps a Sept hike alive - the EUR/USD lift is a USD story. Risk: a hot US CPI Jul 14 reverses it; a hawkish Jul 23 core tone squeezes EUR shorts. Next ECB Jul 23.

strength.tag: Jun +18.2k, unemp 6.5% - but all part-time, goods -43.7k
strength.drivers: May CPI 3.2% (energy-driven; ex-energy 2.1%, trim ~2.0%); Jun jobs +18.2k BEAT ~+10k, unemp fell to 6.5% from 6.6% - but all part-time, goods-producing -43.7k, manufacturing -17k; BoC 2.25%, 5th straight hold; technical recession. The beat broke the USD/CAD uptrend to ~1.4136.
strength.forward: ⚖️ The jobs beat + falling unemployment fired CAD and broke the USD/CAD uptrend, but the internals are hollow and the oil floor is still eroding (~$72); BoC stays sidelined Jul 15. Risk: a hot US CPI re-firms USD/CAD; soft internals + an oil slide re-soften CAD. Next BoC Jul 15.

symbols.why: Downgraded from SHORT: rose to ~1.1420 on the softer USD, not EUR strength; cooling EUR flash vs a USD whose hike bid is leaking - no edge until CPI.
symbols.risk: A hot CPI Jul 14 re-arms the USD short-EUR trade; a hawkish ECB Jul 23 core tone.

symbols.why: Downgraded from LONG: the +18.2k jobs beat + unemp 6.5% broke the July uptrend to ~1.4136, but internals are hollow (part-time, goods -43.7k) and the oil floor still fades - no clean vector.
symbols.risk: A hot US CPI Jul 14 re-firms it; an oil slide + soft-internals re-read weakens CAD again.

symbols.why: Softer US yields let spot ease toward ~161.5; the MoF-backstop asymmetry (Y11.73tn precedent) plus the yield fade give a mild short tilt.
symbols.risk: Yields re-spike on a hot CPI and drag it to 163+; de-escalation restores carry.

symbols.why: Downgraded further: stuck under $72 as Trump's 'Iran reached out' + the glut cap rallies despite live strikes; the Hormuz tail keeps only a thin long bias.
symbols.risk: A Doha breakthrough crushes it; a tanker/Hormuz-closure shock re-arms it.

symbols.why: Soft EUR vs an NZD that just hiked; the dovish statement caps the NZD leg - edge thinned.
symbols.risk: A hawkish NZ Q2 CPI extends it; an EUR core squeeze reverses it.

symbols.why: RBA drifting to hold vs an RBNZ that actually delivered - both legs softened, the edge is thin.
symbols.risk: A hot AU Q2 CPI flips it; NZD's higher-hurdle guidance caps the divergence.

symbols.why: De-escalation lifted the risk beta while softer yields firm JPY - both legs blunted, vector gone.
symbols.risk: A Hormuz re-escalation snaps it down; a hot AU Q2 CPI re-arms AUD.

symbols.why: Soft EUR vs a yield-eased JPY with the MoF backstop - no clean vector.
symbols.risk: A hawkish ECB Jul 23; a yields re-spike.

symbols.why: Dovish-hike NZD vs a firmer JPY - both legs blunted.
symbols.risk: A hot NZ Q2 CPI re-arms NZD; de-escalation lifts carry.

symbols.why: De-escalation beta + softer USD lift it, but a thin hike tail + soft China cap it - two-way.
symbols.risk: A Hormuz shock + a hot US CPI flips it back down.

symbols.why: CAD firmed on jobs, JPY firmed on yields - offsetting, no vector.
symbols.risk: An oil re-spike lifts CAD; a MoF strike lifts JPY.

symbols.why: CAD firmer on jobs while AUD rides risk-on - both legs bid, the edge is thin.
symbols.risk: An oil re-spike re-pins CAD; a hot AU CPI lifts AUD.

symbols.why: Dovish-hike NZD vs a jobs-firmed CAD; both catalysts spent.
symbols.risk: NZ Q2 CPI vs Canada oil/data set the break.

symbols.why: Firm GBP vs a yield-eased JPY - mild long tilt, no momentum.
symbols.risk: A no-warning MoF strike; a yields re-spike.

symbols.why: Firm hawkish GBP (services 3.7%) vs a leaking USD bid - mild long tilt as the dollar softens, but no momentum.
symbols.risk: A hot US CPI re-firms USD; a soft UK activity wave re-splits the MPC.

symbols.why: Post-RBNZ dovish hike vs a softening USD - the soft-USD lift offsets the dovish NZD leg.
symbols.risk: NZ Q2 CPI (~Jul 16) re-opens direction; a hot US CPI.

symbols.why: Two soft legs; neither CB has a near catalyst.
symbols.risk: The AU Q2 CPI late July sets direction.

symbols.why: Firm GBP vs a risk-lifted AUD - flat, headline-hostage.
symbols.risk: De-escalation extends AUD; a hot AU CPI.

symbols.why: Firm GBP vs a delivered-but-dovish RBNZ - no vector post-event.
symbols.risk: NZ Q2 CPI; BoE Jul 30.

symbols.why: The bid is leaking: fading Fed-hike bets + de-escalation dropped DXY 101->100.9 as EUR (57.6% wt) firmed; the SEP caps the downside but Jul 14 CPI is the only trigger that resets direction.
symbols.risk: A hot CPI Jul 14 snaps it back up; a second soft print breaks 100.5.

symbols.why: Softer US yields let JPY firm and spot ease toward ~161.5 - a backstop-asymmetry + yield-fade tilt, still not momentum. (Rises when JPY strengthens - opposite ticket to XXX/JPY.)
symbols.risk: A yields re-spike on a hot CPI drags it lower; de-escalation caps it.

symbols.why: De-escalation hope + oil under $72 restore a mild tailwind, but the index is stretched at record territory (~25,800) and geopolitics is a live two-way.
symbols.risk: A Hormuz re-escalation turns it SHORT; a clean ceasefire restores the melt-up.

symbols.why: Gold firmed back above $4,100 (~$4,115, off Wed's ~$4,030 low) as yields eased and the USD bid leaked; steadies into a little-changed week - trade levels until the Fed path settles.
symbols.risk: A hot CPI re-arms the real-rate selloff; a ceasefire that drops yields AND keeps a haven bid revives the long.

symbols.risk: A high-beta flush lower; follows gold up only if the haven leg re-dominates.

catalysts.note: +18.2k vs ~+10k, unemp 6.5% (down from 6.6%) - beat, but all part-time / goods -43.7k; fired CAD, USD/CAD to ~1.4136.
Mediators (Qatar/Pakistan/Oman/Turkey/Egypt/Saudi) working to reconvene; Trump says Iran 'reached out' - the first hard de-escalation signal drives oil, CAD, JPY, gold.
The 4.2%-regime check - re-arms or kills the Sept hike (~45-50%); the single arbiter now.
Hold; jobs beat but hollow internals + fading oil floor + technical recession.
RBNZ says headline PEAKED at 3.9% - the print validates or breaks the 'higher hurdle' guidance.
Unemployment 4.5% loosening; feeds the Aug 11 RBA calculus.
Hold; core-2.5% is the Sept-hike signal.
Warsh round 2; hold + hawkish minimalism; Sept ~45-50% hangs on the Jul 14 CPI.
Dissent count for GBP after services 3.7%.
Guidance with spot ~161.5 and the MoF zone hot.
Iran: fees 'definitely'; Washington: rejected - with active strikes in between.
3 vessels hit, CENTCOM strikes Jul 8-9, MoU 'over'; every headline moves oil, CAD, JPY, gold.

geopolitics: The kinetic story is still live but the market is now leaning toward de-escalation. CENTCOM's Jul 8-9 strikes (80+ targets, 60+ IRGC boats, in response to three vessels hit in Hormuz) left Trump calling the June-17 MoU 'over,' yet by Friday Trump said Iran had 'reached out to make a deal,' and Qatar, Pakistan, Oman, Turkey, Egypt and Saudi officials are working the phones to reconvene talks. That de-escalation tilt - plus the stacked-crude glut (Iranian barrels plus Saudi ~90% output) - kept WTI soft under $72 (Brent ~$76) and let the rates channel relax: gold firmed back above $4,100 (~$4,115) and the USD bid leaked (DXY 101->100.9) as Fed-hike odds faded. Transmission by currency: the USD's higher-for-longer premium is leaking - the cleanest beneficiary of the war trade is now the weakest link into Jul 14 CPI; CAD firmed on its OWN data (jobs beat + unemployment down) rather than oil, breaking the USD/CAD uptrend even though the internals were hollow; JPY firmed as US yields eased, nudging spot toward ~161.5 (MoF backstop still the deeper story); AUD/NZD caught a de-escalation risk bid; EUR rose on the soft-USD tape, not its own strength; GER40 and gold both firmed on the easing oil-yield pressure. The two-way risk is unchanged and large: a Doha breakthrough crushes what premium remains, while a genuine Hormuz-closure or tanker shock re-arms the whole complex overnight - which is why the board carries no conviction pairs and the oil-linked calls are sized to a whisper.
